factors.name: Risk-on sentiment from Iran deal
factors.summary: US-Iran peace deal ~95% complete with Strait of Hormuz reopening hopes driving broad equity relief. ES +0.4%, NQ +0.6% in Globex, with weaker USD and falling commodity prices supporting tech multiples.

factors.name: Oil crash (-5%) & energy deflation
factors.summary: Brent crude below $100/bbl, WTI surrendering $90 threshold on Hormuz deal expectations. Lower energy inflation supports Fed-hold/cut narrative, bullish for long-duration growth stocks.

factors.name: USD weakness
factors.summary: Dollar sliding on risk-on mood and peace deal optimism (EUR/USD near 1.1640, AUD/USD above 0.7150). Weaker USD historically boosts NQ due to international revenue exposure of mega-caps.

factors.name: Real yields direction unclear
factors.summary: ECB Lagarde flagged June inflation forecast revision upward, and some policymakers signaling June hike unavoidable if Hormuz stays shut. Conflicting signals: oil falling (deflationary) vs ECB hawkishness (higher rates). 10Y move not detailed in news.

factors.name: No major US macro data this week
factors.summary: Today (May 25) is Memorial Day with US markets closed for much of trading. No CPI, PCE, NFP, or FOMC this week—removes near-term rate-shock risk but keeps chop potential.

factors.name: Price action already +0.6% on Globex
factors.summary: NQ rallied 60bps overnight on Iran deal news. Current 4h change only -0.58%, suggesting intraday volatility/chop rather than sustained momentum continuation.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings cycle complete
factors.summary: No mention of upcoming AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA earnings in this news window. Summer earnings drought reduces single-name catalyst risk but also removes upside driver.

factors.name: Crypto correlation positive
factors.summary: Bitcoin bounces on Trump peace deal announcements and weaker USD. BTC-NQ correlation typically positive on risk-on days; crypto tailwind supports sentiment.

factors.name: Deal uncertainty & Trump opacity
factors.summary: Trump stated deal 'isn't even fully negotiated yet' and blockade stays until formal agreement signed. Nuclear/sanctions gaps remain; signing days away. Risk of surprise disappointment.

factors.name: Thin Asian liquidity
factors.summary: Hong Kong markets closed May 25; sparse calendar and holiday-thin tape increase chop risk and reduce conviction on directional moves.

screening.narrative: NAS100 opened the week with +0.6% Globex rally driven by US-Iran peace deal optimism (Strait of Hormuz reopening hopes), falling oil (-5%), and weaker USD—classic risk-on unwind. However, current price action shows only -0.58% 4h change despite overnight strength, suggesting intraday chop and consolidation rather than a sustained push higher. ECB inflation guidance and cautious Trump messaging on deal completion inject uncertainty. Move is already partially in, reducing first-cycle conviction.
catalysts_ahead: Formal US-Iran deal announcement (days to weeks; near-term binary)
ECB June 11 rate decision & Lagarde inflation forecast (late May, could refresh rates narrative)
Next US CPI/PCE (early June; dormant this week due to Memorial Day)
FOMC next meeting (timing TBD; major risk for long-duration equities if hawkish surprise)
Mega-cap earnings restart (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA in coming weeks)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal stalls or Trump backtracks, oil rebounds sharply (+$5-10/bbl possible), pushing real yields higher and compressing NQ multiples. ECB June hike signal could also re-steepen curves and hurt long-duration tech. Thin Asian holiday liquidity increases whipsaw risk; wait for US cash open confirmation of direction. Move already +0.6% overnight—late entry chasers face pullback risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (intraday chop post-gap-up). Avoid chasing above 29,700. Consider dip-buy on retest of 29,200-29,350 if Hormuz deal chatter remains positive and 10Y does not spike. Wait for US cash open (late Monday) to confirm momentum before sizing.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Optimism
factors.summary: Rubio flagged 'pretty solid' framework deal to reopen Hormuz; oil fell 5% to $90.80 WTI, reducing stagflation risk. Lower oil = tailwind for mega-cap tech multiples and consumer spending.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI down 5.5% to near $91, lowest in days. Lower energy costs reduce inflation pressure, improving real yields and reducing Fed tightening risk — direct positive for long-duration Nasdaq names.

factors.name: Risk-On Sentiment / FX Flows
factors.summary: USD/DXY weakened to ~99.05, risk assets rallying globally (Nikkei +new record 65k, Emerging Markets +, AUD/GBP/EUR all higher). Risk-off dollar favors equity inflows to US tech.

factors.name: Fed Rate Cut Hopes
factors.summary: White House adviser Hassett signaled oil drop creates room for Fed rate cuts; new Fed Chair Warsh inheriting dovish bias if geopolitics stabilize. Lower rates = multiple expansion for growth/tech.

factors.name: AI Rally Momentum Intact
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'AI Rally Drives Best Run in Global Momentum Stocks on Record' despite Iran war. Nasdaq 100 components (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, META) remain the primary beneficiary of AI capex cycle.

factors.name: Holiday Liquidity Thinning
factors.summary: US markets closed for Memorial Day; Europe (Germany, France, Switzerland), UK also closed for Whit Monday. Thinner liquidity may cause choppy intraday moves but does not fundamentally change direction.

factors.name: ECB Inflation Revision Risk
factors.summary: Lagarde flagged upward inflation forecast revision in June; some ECB policymakers signal June rate hike unavoidable if Hormuz blockade continues. Hawkish surprise could weigh on European-revenue Nasdaq names.

factors.name: Real Yields & Treasury Curve
factors.summary: No fresh 10Y or TIPS data in this window; oil drop should ease inflation expectations but ECB tightening talk may offset some dovish momentum. Monitor real yields closely — they drive Nasdaq multiple sensitivity.

factors.name: Macro Data Calendar
factors.summary: Asia economic calendar empty today (May 25); no major US data releases until later in week. Reduces near-term catalysts but also reduces downside surprise risk.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Cycle
factors.summary: No earnings releases listed in news window. Next major catalysts (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA earnings, hyperscaler capex guidance) likely weeks out. Sentiment-driven rather than fundamental-driven phase.

key_levels.resistance: 29600–29800 (extended move target if risk-on extends into US open)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in early risk-on phase following weekend optimism on US-Iran deal (Hormuz reopening, oil down 5%, USD weakening). Mega-cap tech benefiting from tailwind: lower oil = reduced stagflation, improved real yields, and Fed cut optionality. However, 4h change is only -0.58% and 24h change -0.02%, suggesting the move is still nascent and not yet priced in. Holiday liquidity thinning (US/UK/Europe closed) may cause chop, and ECB hawkish signals add cross-currents. Prior bias (2026-05-25 00:02) was BUY 6/10 with no confirmation—this is first confirmation cycle.
catalysts_ahead: US market reopening (regular hours Tue 5/28 post-Memorial Day)
ECB Governing Council meeting (June 11) — Lagarde upward inflation revision expected
Fed FOMC minutes (May 28 or later) — rate cut vs. hold/hike signals
Major tech earnings: AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL (dates TBD, likely June)
CPI/PCE inflation prints (next window: early June)
Oil inventory data (weekly EIA reports Tue/Wed)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal negotiations stall, oil rebounds, or Hormuz blockade re-escalates, the entire risk-on narrative collapses — Nasdaq could quickly reverse. ECB hawkish surprise (June rate hike odds rising) could also trigger DXY rebound and real yield spike, pressuring long-duration growth. Thinner holiday liquidity may also whipsaw early moves; wait for US cash market open Tue for confirmation.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (fresh but gaining momentum on risk-on). Enter long on pullback to 29350–29400 support with tight stops below 29250; avoid chasing extended moves into thin holiday session. Ideal re-entry is after US market reopens Tue with higher volume confirmation.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Progress
factors.summary: Multiple sources report US-Iran framework deal ~95% complete to reopen Strait of Hormuz. Rubio says 'pretty solid thing on the table.' Oil down ~5%, risk-on sentiment boosted across global markets (Nikkei tops 65,000 first time, Asia stocks climb).

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI tumbles 5%+ toward $90.80 on Hormuz reopening hopes. Lower energy costs = easing inflation pressures = more room for Fed cuts. White House adviser Hassett explicitly said oil drop 'may create room for Fed rate cut.'

factors.name: Fed Rate Cut Expectations Reviving
factors.summary: White House adviser Hassett confident oil price drop creates space for Fed cuts. Counterbalances recent hawkish FOMC positioning. Lower terminal rate expectations = multiple expansion for long-duration tech.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: DXY weakens to near 99.00 on risk-on mood and Iran deal hopes. NAS100 components derive heavy international revenue; weaker dollar = earnings tailwind and valuation support.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: Lower oil = lower inflation expectations = lower real yields ahead. Tech multiples extremely sensitive to real rate moves; easing oil pressure removes key headwind that drove yields to 2-year highs.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Global Equity Rally
factors.summary: Asia stocks rally hard (Nikkei +65k milestone, emerging markets rise). Futures show ES +0.4%, NQ +0.6% at Globex open. Broad risk-on environment favors growth/tech.

factors.name: AI Momentum Stocks
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'AI Rally Drives Best Run in Global Momentum Stocks on Record' despite Iran war worries. Core tech narrative remains intact; geopolitical de-escalation removes overhang.

factors.name: Inflation Relief Catalyst
factors.summary: India raises fuel prices 4th time in May, but global oil collapse reverses this trajectory. Disinflationary impulse from energy = Fed less constrained = growth stock positive.

factors.name: Memorial Day Holiday / Thin Liquidity
factors.summary: US markets closed Monday, UK closed, some European markets closed. Light volumes may exaggerate moves but also limit conviction. Hong Kong out thins Asian liquidity.

factors.name: Recent NDX Price Action
factors.summary: NAS100 essentially flat (-0.02% 24h, -0.58% 4h). No extended move yet; positioned neutrally ahead of catalyst. Phase 1 opportunity.

factors.name: ECB Inflation Revision / Hawkish Tilt
factors.summary: Lagarde flags upward revision to 2025 inflation forecast at June 11 meeting; ECB policymakers signal June hike likely if Hormuz stayed shut. European rates rising = global yield competition, but Hormuz deal mitigates this risk.

factors.name: China Trade Curbs / HK Outflows
factors.summary: China crackdown on cross-border trading may affect $32B Hong Kong assets per Citic. Adds regulatory uncertainty for tech with China exposure, though less direct NAS100 impact than A-shares.

factors.name: Memory / Semiconductor Caution
factors.summary: BlueBox Asset Mgmt warns 'In the long run, [memory] is a pretty dreadful industry' amid AI boom. Cyclical concerns for semis, though offset by broader AI tailwinds and Huawei 1.4nm chip law news (competitive but not immediate threat).

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 positioned for bullish breakout as US-Iran peace framework (~95% complete) triggers sharp oil decline (-5%), rekindling Fed rate cut hopes and compressing real yields—the two critical drivers for long-duration tech multiples. White House explicitly linking oil relief to Fed policy space. Risk-on sentiment surging globally (Nikkei record, Asia rallies, NQ futures +0.6%) while NDX itself has not yet moved materially (-0.02% 24h), presenting Phase 1 entry. Memorial Day thin liquidity may amplify move but reduces conviction slightly. ECB hawkish shift is counterbalance, but Hormuz deal removes the core inflation driver that necessitated it.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal signing (days away per officials; full text/sanctions details critical)
Fed Chair Warsh first major speech/testimony (new appointment, policy stance unclear)
ECB June 11 meeting (Lagarde confirmed inflation revision, possible hike)
Oil inventory data (confirm supply normalization post-Hormuz)
End-of-month flows and Memorial Day return liquidity Tuesday
Any mega-cap tech earnings (NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL not flagged but always NDX catalyst)
confirmation.risk_warning: Iran deal collapse or delay (Trump said 'not fully negotiated,' blockade stays until signed) would reverse oil drop and reignite inflation fears, spiking yields and hammering tech. ECB June hike despite Hormuz progress could pull global yields higher. Thin holiday liquidity cuts both ways—gains may evaporate quickly. Confidence capped at 7 due to deal execution risk and holiday session volatility.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Fresh Phase 1 setup; consider long on any dip toward 29,300-29,350 support or on confirmed breakout above 29,600 with volume, targeting 29,800-30,000. Avoid chasing if gap-up exceeds +1.5% at Tuesday open.

reason: FRESH high-conf read (7/10) — informational only, needs confirmation
